steps.title: Translate the total shrub constraint
steps.content: The phrase no more than 268 total shrubs means the sum cannot exceed 268. The phrase no more than translates to the inequality symbol ≤. Therefore, the total number of shrubs is: $$h + w \le 268$$ This eliminates choices C and D, which use ≥ instead.

steps.title: Translate the holly-to-weigela relationship
steps.content:
The phrase the number of hollies will be at most three times the number of weigelas means hollies cannot exceed 3 times the weigela count.

The phrase at most translates to ≤. Therefore: $$h \le 3w$$ Choice B — $h + w \le 268$ $3h \ge w$ has $3h \ge w$ (equivalent to $h \ge \frac{w}{3}$), which says hollies are at least one-third the weigela count — the opposite of what we need.

Choice D — $h + w \ge 268$ $3h \ge w$ also has this reversed relationship. Only Choice A — $h + w \le 268$ $h \le 3w$ has both $h + w \le 268$ and $h \le 3w$. That matches Choice A — $h + w \le 268$ $h \le 3w$.
